prompt: This Singleton implementation prints [1] first and [1] second — the logCount never increments past 1. What is the bug?
choices: logCount must be declared static to persist across method calls
getInstance() unconditionally creates a new Logger on every call — the null-check guard is missing
The method should be synchronized to be thread-safe
private constructors cannot be called from static methods
explanation: A correct Singleton only creates the instance when it is null: if (instance == null) instance = new Logger(); Without the guard, every call to getInstance() creates a fresh Logger with logCount=0. Fix: add the null check so the instance is created exactly once.
